meta.description: Enforcer career questline — from street thug to elite private military commander, 100 nodes spanning protection rackets, corporate security contracts, gang wars, and the moral weight of violence as profession

enf_001.title: The Recruitment
text: The job posting is spray-painted on a wall in Block 9: MUSCLE NEEDED. GOOD PAY. ASK FOR IRONJAW AT THE RED DOOR. No address, no contact number, no corporate branding. In the Lower District, the best jobs are the ones that don't advertise through official channels.

The Red Door is exactly what it sounds like — a reinforced security door painted arterial red, set into the side of a converted factory in the industrial quarter. Two men stand outside, both built like surplus construction materials given human form.

Inside, the factory floor has been converted into a training space. Heavy bags hang from ceiling beams. A sparring ring occupies the center. Weapons racks line the far wall — stun batons, riot shields, ceramic-plated body armor.

Captain Rex 'Ironjaw' Maddox sits behind a metal desk, reviewing dossiers. He's a massive man — two meters of muscle and scar tissue, his jaw rebuilt with titanium after an explosion took the original. When he speaks, the metal gives his voice a resonant, mechanical edge.

choices.text: "I can fight. I can take hits. And I don't ask questions."
"I'm smart muscle. I think before I swing."

enf_002.title: The Grunt's Promise
text: Ironjaw studies you the way a butcher studies a carcass — assessing yield, structural integrity, usefulness. His titanium jaw clicks once, a mechanical tic that passes for contemplation.

"Everyone says they don't ask questions. Most are lying. The ones who aren't are dangerous — not tough, but willing to do anything without thinking. I need people who choose not to ask. There's a difference."
He slides a contract across the desk. Protection details. Escort work. Occasional enforcement. The pay is better than anything else available to someone with your skill set.
"You start as a Grunt. Bottom rung. You do what your squad leader tells you. You protect the client. You don't start fights, but you finish them. Clear?"

enf_003.title: The Thinking Fighter
text: Ironjaw's metal jaw shifts into something that might be a smile. "Smart muscle. That's rarer than you'd think. Plenty of people can take a punch. The ones who can assess, identify, and neutralize before the fight starts — those make Lieutenant."
He leans back. "But you start at the bottom like everyone else. Grunt work. Protection details. The boring business of standing between a client and anyone who wants to hurt them."
He slides a contract across the desk. "Terms are non-negotiable. The pay is fair. The work is dangerous. And I don't tolerate operators who freelance on my time."

choices.text: Sign the contract. Best opportunity you'll get.

enf_004.title: First Detail
text: Your first assignment: escort a mid-level merchant named Priya Vasari through the market district while she collects debts. Your squad leader is Kess — five years with Ironjaw's outfit, hard as rebar, with a prosthetic left arm she claims she lost in a 'disagreement.'
"Stand there. Look intimidating. If someone runs, trip them. If someone swings, block it. Don't hit anyone unless I tell you to. The client's safety is the only thing that matters."
The work is tedious. Eight hours of standing in doorways. Your feet hurt. Your back aches. Nothing happens.

choices.text: Stay alert. Quiet assignments are the most dangerous.
Ask Kess about the outfit's hierarchy.

enf_005.title: The Quiet Breaks
text: Your instinct pays off. On the sixth stop, a debtor named Harlan comes out with a pipe wrench. You're already moving — you noticed his body language shift three seconds before he acted. The subtle weight transfer. The shoulder drop as his hand found the wrench.

You intercept the swing with your forearm, absorb the impact, and redirect Harlan into the wall. Not hard enough to break anything, but hard enough to communicate that the violence calculus has changed.
Kess has her stun baton out but doesn't need it. Priya adjusts her ledger: "Add the medical costs to his tab."

choices.text: Help Harlan up. "Pay what you owe. Next time I won't be gentle."
Keep him pinned. Let Priya negotiate from strength.

enf_006.title: The Hierarchy
text: Kess explains between stops. "Four ranks. Grunt — that's you. Guard — you run your own details. Lieutenant — you command a squad. Commander — military-grade operations, corporate contracts."
"And Ironjaw?"

"Ironjaw is Ironjaw. Used to be corporate security — Meridian Solutions, elite tier. Something happened. He doesn't talk about it. Now he runs the best independent security operation in the Lower District."
She pauses. "He's particular about loyalty. People who freelance — take side jobs, sell information — they don't get fired. They disappear."

choices.text: "Understood. I'm here for the long haul."

enf_007.title: Earning Stripes
text: Weeks pass. You work details — merchants, money runners, occasionally someone important enough for a two-person escort. You learn the geography of threat: which districts are safe, which corridors are ambush points, which intersections have sight lines that make them killing grounds.
You learn to read people. The way someone walks tells you whether they're armed. Eye movement, breathing patterns, micro-expressions — the grammar of violence.
Kess starts trusting you with more responsibility. She lets you take point on a detail. It's a test. You know it's a test.
The detail goes clean. Kess nods — the highest praise in her vocabulary.

choices.text: Ask about the enforcement side of the business.

enf_008.title: Ironjaw's Past
text: Kess's expression closes like a bulkhead sealing. "That's not a question you ask. Not yet." A softening. "Ironjaw didn't leave Meridian. Meridian left him. They were doing something with cortical stacks and people who couldn't say no. Ironjaw objected. Their response was the explosion that took his jaw."
"They tried to kill him?"
"They tried to make an example. He survived, rebuilt his face with salvaged titanium, and spent three years building an outfit to protect the people Meridian was exploiting."
She looks at you directly. "That's why loyalty matters. This outfit is built on the idea that protection means something."

choices.text: "I won't ask again until he's ready to tell me."

enf_009.title: The Warehouse Job
text: Your first real test: secure a warehouse during a high-value exchange. Two parties, neither trusting the other. The warehouse is a tactical nightmare — multiple entry points, poor lighting, elevated catwalks with firing positions, cargo containers creating blind spots.
The client — Odera, a medical supplies trader — wants the exchange in the center. Open ground. "Neutral territory."
You look at the catwalks above, the shadows in the corners, and think: neutral territory is a fiction. Every space favors someone.

choices.text: Rearrange the meeting point — use containers as cover.
Scout the catwalks first. Secure the high ground.

enf_010.title: The Other Business
text: The outfit has two revenue streams. Protection details are the public face. The other side is enforcement: collecting debts, securing territory, the careful application of intimidation.

A veteran named Drake shows you the ropes. Quiet man, hands like shovels. "We don't hurt people for fun. We hurt people because the alternative is chaos. The Lower District doesn't have police. We're the enforcement mechanism."
He pauses. "Some people we visit deserve it. Some don't. The job doesn't ask you to make that judgment."

choices.text: "There have to be lines. What won't we do?"

enf_011.title: Tactical Advantage
text: You spend two hours rearranging cargo containers, creating a meeting space with cover for your client and limited escape routes for the other party. The other party's security lead — Voss — recognizes it immediately.
"Smart setup," she says. "Who taught you?"
"Experience."
The exchange proceeds without incident. Nobody dies. In the Lower District, that's a resounding success.

enf_012.title: High Ground
text: You find two snipers on the catwalks. Independents, hired to sabotage the exchange. Nobody scouts catwalks in advance. You come up the maintenance ladder silently and put your boot on the first sniper's neck.
"Two options. Leave quietly with your equipment and your health. Or leave loudly."
Both choose quiet. The exchange proceeds without anyone below knowing how close they came to a massacre.

choices.text: Report the snipers to Ironjaw. Someone wanted this deal to fail.
Keep the detail quiet. Use it as leverage later.

enf_013.title: Collections
text: First collection: a food vendor in Block 12, three weeks behind on payments. Not because he can't pay — because he's testing whether the enforcement is real.
Drake stands silently while Sully makes excuses. Business slow. Supply chain disrupted. Daughter sick. When the excuses run out, Drake says one sentence: "The money. Today."
Sully produces the money. He had it the whole time. The enforcement wasn't about violence — it was about certainty.

choices.text: Ask Drake about the harder collections.
Tell Sully you'll check in next week. Build a relationship.

enf_014.title: The Lines
text: Drake considers your question. "No children. No one who can't fight back. No permanent damage unless Ironjaw authorizes it personally. No killing unless self-defense or sanctioned."
"We don't take contracts against medical facilities. Don't target water infrastructure. Don't work for anyone dealing in cortical stack trafficking. Those are Ironjaw's rules, non-negotiable."
"And if someone crosses those lines?"
"Then they're not part of the outfit anymore. And the outfit deals with former members thoroughly."

choices.text: Good enough. Let's get to work.

enf_015.title: Ironjaw's Assessment
text: Ironjaw reviews your report. Click. Click. "Clean work." Two words carrying more weight than a speech. Clean means professional.
"You're being wasted on escort details. Starting next week, tactical rotation. Higher-risk, better pay, more autonomy. Report to Kess directly."

"But understand: tactical rotation means situations where the clean option isn't available. Where the choice is between bad and worse. I expect you to choose the option that protects the client and preserves the outfit's reputation. In that order."

enf_016.title: Dangerous Knowledge
text: You keep the sniper information to yourself. Knowledge is leverage. Someone hired those snipers, and knowing who gives you power.

Two days later, Ironjaw calls you in. "The catwalk snipers. Voss's team found shell casings. She reported it to me. Want to tell me why I'm hearing about a threat in my operation from someone else's security lead?"
The temperature in the room drops. Ironjaw's trust is not something you can bank.

choices.text: Explain your reasoning — you were investigating.

enf_017.title: The Hard Ones
text: "The hard ones are the people who genuinely can't pay. Not won't — can't. Borrowed because they were desperate, now more desperate."
"What do we do?"
"Ironjaw's rule: report them. Sometimes he restructures the debt. Sometimes writes it off. He loses money, but says the outfit's reputation isn't built on squeezing people who have nothing."
"Other outfits take what they're owed in flesh. Ironjaw's approach costs more short-term. Long-term, people trust us. Trust is worth more than any individual debt."

choices.text: Continue learning. There's depth here you didn't expect.

enf_018.title: Building Trust
text: You come back to check on Sully every week. Not to collect — just to check in. He tells you about his business, the supply chains, the margins. Within a month, he pays on time without being asked.
Not because he fears you, but because you've become part of his business reality — a regular presence representing the system working as intended.
Drake notices. He starts sending you on the relationship collections — the ones where intimidation isn't working.

choices.text: This approach works. People respond to respect.

enf_019.title: The Gang War
text: The Iron Chain and Bone Saints are escalating. Three dead in the last week, including a bystander. Ironjaw assembles the tactical team — six operators, you among them.
A medical clinic in the contested zone needs protection. The doctor, Adira, refuses to close. "The wounded need treatment regardless of which gang they belong to."
"Protect the clinic. Protect the doctor. Protect the patients. We are neutral. We don't engage combatants unless they threaten the clinic. This is the kind of job that makes or breaks reputations."

choices.text: "What are the rules of engagement if the gangs test us?"
"How long is the contract? This could be a siege."

enf_020.title: The Clinic
text: The clinic: a converted apartment on the boundary between Iron Chain and Bone Saints territory. Dr. Adira greets you with exhausted focus. "I don't care about your guns. Can you keep the door open? The moment I close it, people die."
You set up a perimeter. Two operators on each entrance, two rotating patrol. The clinic's location makes it defensible but also a pressure point.
The first test comes within hours. A Bone Saints runner appears, wounded, pursued by Iron Chain enforcers who stop at your perimeter and stare.

choices.text: Let the runner in. The clinic is neutral ground.
Block the pursuit. The perimeter is absolute.

enf_021.title: Neutral Ground
text: You wave the runner through. Dr. Adira treats his wounds while Iron Chain enforcers watch from beyond the perimeter.
Their leader approaches. "That's our kill. He was running product through our territory."
"He's a patient now. The clinic doesn't recognize gang affiliations. When he's treated, he leaves through the back. Your dispute resumes outside our perimeter."
He considers. His crew shifts, hands near weapons. You stand your ground — not aggressively but absolutely.
He leaves. Dr. Adira looks at you with cautious hope.

choices.text: Maintain the perimeter. Both gangs will test it again.

enf_022.title: The Perimeter Holds
text: You step forward, putting yourself between the Iron Chain enforcers and the clinic. Behind you, your team adjusts — covering angles, establishing fields of fire.
"The perimeter is the perimeter." Your voice is level, controlled.
The lead enforcer performs the arithmetic of violence and arrives at a number he doesn't like. He spits on the ground and walks away.
The perimeter held. Today. Tomorrow it will be tested again.

choices.text: Reinforce the perimeter. This is just beginning.

enf_023.title: Night Watch
text: The night shift. At 0300, three figures approach the rear exit — carrying containers. Not weapons. Medical supplies. Anonymous donors who don't want either gang to know they're helping.
You let them through. Three crates of antibiotics, bandages, and surgical supplies deposited without a word.

choices.text: Tell Dr. Adira about the donation.

enf_024.title: The Siege
text: Day four. Both gangs escalating outside your perimeter. Then the Bone Saints send a delegation — not fighters. A woman named Sister Ash, unarmed.
"We want to use the clinic as a ceasefire point. Neutral ground for negotiations with the Iron Chain. Dr. Adira's neutrality gives us cover."
The request is reasonable. It's also dangerous — a negotiation venue becomes a higher-value target.

choices.text: Refuse. The clinic treats wounds, not politics.

enf_025.title: The Ceasefire
text: You set terms: no weapons inside, maximum three reps per side, your team controls access, Dr. Adira has authority to halt negotiations. Both gangs accept.
Three hours later, a preliminary agreement: seventy-two-hour ceasefire, boundary adjustment, and the clinic established as permanent neutral ground.
It's not peace. But it's the absence of war, which in the Lower District is the closest approximation available.

choices.text: Report to Ironjaw. This changes everything.

enf_026.title: Holding the Line
text: "The clinic is a medical facility, not a conference room." Sister Ash studies you. "You're making a mistake. More fighting means more wounded means the clinic gets overwhelmed."

She's not wrong. But your contract is clear. The fighting continues another week. Dr. Adira starts sleeping in her treatment room. You maintain the perimeter, professional and effective and increasingly aware that being right and being helpful aren't always the same thing.

enf_027.title: Promotion: Guard
text: Ironjaw promotes you in his office — new comm frequency, "You're Guard rank now. Don't make me regret it." No ceremony. The outfit doesn't do pomp.
Guard rank means autonomy. You pick your assignments, run your own details. Pay doubles. Risk triples.
The rank insignia is a ceramic pin shaped like a shield, worn on the collar. It marks you as someone the outfit trusts with independent judgment.
"Guard rank comes with a choice. Focus on protection — security, escort, defense. Or move into enforcement — collections, territory, the harder edge. Both paths lead to Lieutenant."

choices.text: Focus on protection. Security is what the district needs.

enf_028.title: The Protector's Path
text: You choose protection. Your first independent contract: a water treatment engineer named Ito who maintains the district's secondary water system. Ten thousand people depend on him.
Someone wants Ito dead. Threats, dead animals at his door, a shot through his window. He suspects a corporate water company buying up Lower District rights.
"If they kill me, they control the water. If they control the water, they control the district."

choices.text: Set up safe houses. Mobile protection, unpredictable patterns.
Investigate the threats. Find the source.

enf_029.title: The Enforcer's Path
text: You choose enforcement. Drake mentors you. First job: a territory dispute. A market in Block 8 has been under the outfit's protection for two years. A new crew — the Rust Vipers — is pressuring merchants to switch.
"The Vipers are aggressive," Drake warns. "Young crew, hungry, willing to go further than we are. If you handle this with just muscle, they'll escalate. Be smarter."

choices.text: Talk to the Vipers' leader. Find out what they want.
Show force. The outfit's territory is non-negotiable.

enf_030.title: The Safe House
text: Three safe houses, rotating on irregular schedules. Two assassination attempts fail. You recover military-grade ammunition — someone is spending real money.
On the third night, you catch a surveillance drone — Meridian Solutions branding. Inside the memory core: targeting coordinates for all three safe houses.
Someone inside the outfit leaked Ito's location.

choices.text: Report the leak to Ironjaw immediately.

enf_031.title: Following the Thread
text: The trail leads to Hydra Water Solutions — a corporate subsidiary quietly purchasing water rights. Ito's system is the last obstacle to monopoly.
"Hydra is a Meridian subsidiary," Ironjaw says. "This isn't just about water. This is about control."
"Protect Ito. But also — gather evidence. Everything about Hydra's operations. If we fight Meridian, we need ammunition that doesn't come in shell casings."

enf_032.title: The Viper's Head
text: Scarlet — the Vipers' leader — meets you alone except for a knife she doesn't bother hiding. "Your outfit is old school. We offer the same, faster and cheaper."
"Not when your cheaper option involves breaking kneecaps."
"Fair point. But your ethics are expensive. Some merchants would prefer cheaper protection with fewer principles."
She's not wrong. The outfit's moral code is a competitive disadvantage.

choices.text: Offer an alliance. Vipers handle rough work, outfit handles premium.
Make it clear: Block 8 is ours. Back off.

enf_033.title: Show of Force
text: Four operators in tactical gear, positioned at the market's cardinal points, communicating via encrypted comms. The Vipers' presence shrinks visibly.
The merchants reconsider switching. Visible competence is its own advertisement.
But Scarlet watches from a rooftop. She's not scared. She's learning.

choices.text: The Vipers will adapt. You need a longer-term strategy.

enf_034.title: The Mole Hunt
text: Ironjaw authorizes a mole hunt. You and Kess compartmentalize information, feed different details to different operators, watch who leaks what.
After two weeks, three suspects. Patel — spending above his pay grade. Torres — making encrypted calls during off-hours. And someone you never expected: Drake.

choices.text: Confront Drake directly. He deserves that respect.
Set a trap. Feed each suspect unique info.

enf_035.title: Shadow Investigation
text: You investigate alone. Three sleepless nights monitoring comms. Finally: a burst transmission, military-grade encryption — Meridian standard. The origin point: Kess's comm unit.
Your stomach drops. Either she's the mole, or someone is using her equipment.

choices.text: Go to Ironjaw with the evidence.
Confront Kess privately. Give her a chance to explain.

enf_036.title: Alliance or War
text: Scarlet proposes: outfit gets premium contracts, Vipers handle street-level. Sixty-forty split, outfit's favor. Vipers get training, equipment, reputation.
"In two years, we're professionals instead of street thugs. That's worth the forty percent."

choices.text: Counter-offer: Vipers integrate entirely. No separate crew.

enf_037.title: The Line Drawn
text: You deliver the message. Scarlet nods. The Vipers withdraw from Block 8 within twenty-four hours. Too clean. Too professional.
Drake's assessment: "She's flanking us. She gave up Block 8 because she's already in Block 11 and 13. Surrounding our territory instead of contesting it."
Scarlet isn't a street thug. She's a strategist, and you fell for the tactical withdrawal that becomes strategic encirclement.

enf_038.title: Confrontation
text: The mole is Torres. The trap caught him clean — unique safe house coordinates fed to him appeared in Meridian's intelligence.
Torres doesn't deny it. "Meridian offered me a cortical stack. Real immortality. Five years of intelligence for a lifetime. Infinite lifetimes. You'd have done the same thing."
The room is very quiet.

choices.text: "Nobody's worth infinity if the price is betraying people who trusted you."
Say nothing. Let Ironjaw handle his soldier.

enf_039.title: The Trap
text: Three pieces of false intel to three suspects. Forty-eight hours later, a Meridian recon team appears at Torres's location. Clean, undeniable confirmation.
Torres trained alongside you. Watched your back on the clinic detail. And the whole time, selling everything to the corporation that tried to kill Ironjaw.

enf_040.title: Kess's Truth
text: Kess listens, then: "Torres borrowed my comm unit two weeks ago. Said his was malfunctioning. One day was enough to clone the encryption keys. He's been piggybacking my signal."
Torres cloned her authentication, creating a false trail pointing to the most loyal operator — buying him time.
"I'll kill him," Kess says.
"We bring this to Ironjaw. By the book."

choices.text: Take the evidence to Ironjaw together.

enf_041.title: Strategic Thinking
text: You present Ironjaw with a strategic analysis. The outfit is strong in core territory but vulnerable at edges. The security market is growing faster than you can service it.
"What do you recommend?" The first time he's asked your opinion on strategy. He's evaluating you for something beyond Guard rank.

enf_042.title: Absorption
text: "No separate crew. You join the outfit. Your people train under our standards, follow our rules, earn rank like everyone else."
Long pause. "I keep my people together as a unit. My squad. And I want Guard rank."
"You'll earn Guard rank. Same as everyone else."
Another pause. "Deal."

enf_043.title: Justice in the Lower District
text: Torres is dealt with. Not violence — exile. Stripped of rank, equipment, identity. His face distributed to every contact. In the Lower District, where reputation is survival, Torres ceases to exist.
"Killing him would be simpler," Kess says.
"Killing him would make us like Meridian," Ironjaw replies. "The distinction matters especially when it's harder."
A week later: "You handled the mole situation well. You showed judgment, not just skill."

enf_044.title: The Corporate Contract
text: A sealed envelope — actual paper, actual seal. A contract from Nexus Industries: six months, twenty operators, military-grade equipment. Payment that would fund the outfit for a year.
"This is corporate work," Ironjaw says. "Different rules. I want you to run the assessment. Evaluate the contract, visit the facility, determine whether this is an opportunity or a trap."

choices.text: Accept the assessment. This could elevate the outfit.
Express concerns. Corporate money comes with chains.

enf_045.title: Specialization
text: You argue for focusing on what the outfit does best: high-value protection, complex operations. Let small crews handle market protection.
"There's a middle path," Ironjaw says. "Specialize upward but maintain a community tier. Corporate money subsidizes community protection."
Elegant but expensive. You'll need more operators, better equipment, and a dual organizational structure.

enf_046.title: The Nexus Facility
text: The Nexus facility produces cybernetic components — prosthetics, neural interfaces, cortical stack housings. The facility manager, Chen, walks you through clean rooms and assembly lines. Everything is professional, humane.
You notice things Chen doesn't mention: biometric scanners, electromagnetic shielding, an armed response team in the sub-basement that doesn't appear on any facility map.
Nexus has existing security. What they want from the outfit isn't protection — it's something else.

choices.text: Accept the tour. Complete the assessment.

enf_047.title: The Corporate Concern
text: "Corporate contracts are golden handcuffs, Captain. Nexus will want control."
"You're not wrong. But we need the revenue. Payroll, equipment, medical — costs are outpacing income. Without corporate money, we contract."
The math is simple: grow with corporate money or shrink without it.

choices.text: "Then we negotiate hard. Take the money, keep the independence."

enf_048.title: Beneath the Surface
text: Chen's smile flickers at the sub-basement mention. "The internal team handles classified operations. Your role is perimeter and external threats. Compartmentalization."
Compartmentalization isn't just security — it's an opacity tool. Whatever Nexus is doing down there, they're paying the outfit to look outward so nobody looks inward.

choices.text: Take the contract but investigate on your own.

enf_049.title: Promotion: Lieutenant
text: "I'm promoting you. Effective immediately, you're Lieutenant. You'll command the Nexus detail and a second squad for Lower District operations."
"Me? I'm Guard rank, Captain."
"Which is why I'm promoting you."
The ceramic pin changes from shield to crossed sword-and-shield. Command and protection, united.

choices.text: Accept the promotion. Time to lead.

enf_050.title: Ironjaw's Decision
text: "Meridian used the same architecture," Ironjaw says quietly. "External security looking outward, internal team handling work that couldn't be seen."
"We take the contract. Not for the money. For access. If Nexus is running something dirty, I want operators on the inside. Not for security. For intelligence."
"You want me to spy on our own client?"
"I want you to protect the Lower District. Sometimes protection means knowing what's behind the locked doors."

enf_051.title: The Secret Investigation
text: By day, professional perimeter security. By night, probing the sub-basement. Weeks of mapping blind spots, timing rotations. Finally, a maintenance conduit bypassing primary security.
Inside: rows of medical pods, each containing a human body in stasis. Cortical stacks exposed, connected to a data harvesting array.
Nexus isn't manufacturing components. They're reverse-engineering them from living subjects who cannot consent.

choices.text: Get evidence. Bring it to Ironjaw.

enf_052.title: Lieutenant's Command
text: Command is different from execution. You organize the Nexus detail into three shifts. Professional perimeter security. But you're watching for more than external threats.
Three months in: late-night medical equipment deliveries. Biohazard waste from an unregistered contractor. People entering the sub-basement who never leave through monitored exits.

choices.text: Time to see what's in the sub-basement.
Brief your team. This might require a tactical op.

enf_053.title: The Discovery
text: Sixteen medical pods. Lower District residents in induced coma, cortical stacks exposed and connected to data extraction arrays. Recruited through the employment program, then disappeared — the homeless, the unregistered, people nobody counts.
A factory of human suffering, hidden beneath a factory of prosthetic limbs.
You take photographs. Copy data. Catalog everything with methodical precision.

enf_054.title: Direct Action
text: You can't wait. You disable the extraction array, trigger emergency pod release, and activate the fire alarm. The subjects wake slowly — months of stasis have atrophied muscles and clouded minds.
Sixteen people, blinking in streetlight they haven't seen in months. Behind you, Nexus's failsafe burns the evidence along with the infrastructure.

enf_055.title: Team Briefing
text: You brief your most trusted operators. "Our client is conducting human experimentation in a sub-basement we're being paid not to look at. At least sixteen subjects."
Kess's expression hardens. Drake's fists curl.
"When do we move?" Drake asks.

enf_056.title: The Reckoning
text: Ironjaw receives the evidence in silence. No clicking jaw. Empty face. "Meridian did this. Different company, same practice. I thought I stopped them."
"We terminate the Nexus contract. Release the evidence everywhere. And extract every subject tonight."
"That's war, Captain."
"Then it's war. This is what the outfit exists for. Not contracts. Not revenue. Protection. Real protection. The kind that costs everything."

choices.text: "I'm with you, Captain. All the way."

enf_057.title: The Extraction
text: The outfit's finest operation. Twenty-eight operators, coordinated precision. You command from the security center. Kess leads extraction. Drake handles perimeter.
Nexus's internal security — six operators, well-armed but unprepared — are neutralized without casualties. Non-lethal. Ironjaw insisted.
By dawn, subjects are in medical care at Dr. Adira's expanded field hospital. Evidence disseminated. Facility empty.

enf_058.title: Fallout
text: Nexus responds legally — lawyers, cease-and-desist, a media campaign painting the outfit as violent extremists. But the sixteen subjects telling their stories are more convincing than any press release.
Community support surges. Small donations from people with almost nothing. Legal pressure freezes the outfit's accounts. Operating becomes harder. But not impossible.
The outfit goes partially underground. The Lower District provides cover, safe houses, intelligence.

choices.text: Go on the offensive against Nexus.

enf_059.title: The Long Game
text: Weeks of legal harassment and financial pressure. The outfit survives because the district needs it. Contracts paid in cash, barter, favors.
Three months in, Nexus blinks. Media attention, community resistance, and a leaked internal audit showing the board knew. Injunctions dropped. Accounts unfrozen. Nexus withdraws entirely.
Not a victory. A reprieve. In the Lower District, reprieves pass for victories.

enf_060.title: Counterattack
text: Not violence — disruption. Supply chains interrupted. Contractors warned off. Worker cooperatives replacing Nexus facilities.
Two months: Nexus hemorrhaging money. Workers staging walkouts. The board cuts losses and withdraws.
The strongest force isn't military — it's economic.

enf_061.title: Promotion: Commander
text: "Commander means you make decisions I don't review. I trust that judgment because you've earned it — not with obedience, but with the ability to think independently and act ethically under pressure."
New comm unit — military-grade encryption, quantum-secured.
The pin changes to sword crossed with shield. Command and protection, united.

choices.text: "I'll honor the rank and the mission."

enf_062.title: The Commander's View
text: You see the entire system: forty-seven clients, twelve territory agreements, thirty-six operators. Budget tight but sustainable.
The biggest challenge is existential. Corporate investment increasing, bringing money and infrastructure but also control. New threats wear suits as often as weapons.

enf_063.title: Modernization
text: Cyber-security, corporate intelligence analysis, legal consultation, media relations. You recruit specialists: a former corporate analyst, a media consultant, a legal advocate.
The outfit evolves. Protection now means defending against hostile takeovers and exploitative contracts as well as physical violence.

enf_064.title: Community Roots
text: Every operator spends time as a resident — attending meetings, helping with repairs, training self-defense classes. Community boards give residents voice in their own protection.
Protection becomes a shared project, not a transaction.

enf_065.title: The Meridian Return
text: Meridian Solutions returns — not with soldiers but contracts. "Community uplift." Free cortical stacks. Medical clinics. Price: a ninety-nine-year development lease.
"It's the same play," Ironjaw says. "They'll invest, build, provide — and when the district depends on them, they'll own it. Every clinic a data harvesting point. Every stack installation a leash."

enf_066.title: The Public Campaign
text: Every piece of Nexus evidence republished. Community meetings become forums. Meridian responds with counter-narratives and legal action.
The district divides. Some want investment — free stacks, medical care. Others remember the sub-basement pods. The division is Meridian's weapon.

enf_067.title: The Alternative
text: Community-owned clinics. Training programs without corporate indenture. A mutual aid network. Meridian responds by underpricing — offering services free, knowing unlimited capital outlasts cooperative budgets.
Economic warfare. Meridian has the bigger army. But you have trust.

enf_068.title: The Final Battle
text: The confrontation: a community vote on Meridian's proposal. Ironjaw speaks at the final meeting, removes the titanium plate. Underneath: scar tissue, surgical steel.
"This is what they gave me for saying no. Free cortical stacks. And the freedom to become their property."
The vote is close. Heartbreakingly close. But the district votes no.

choices.text: The battle is won. The war continues.

enf_069.title: Meridian's Retreat
text: Meridian withdraws. Community cooperatives survive and grow. Clinics expand. Training programs graduate.
The outfit evolves. Less muscle, more coordination. The transformation Ironjaw envisioned is finally, imperfectly, becoming real.

choices.text: The outfit becomes what the district needs.

enf_070.title: Legacy Planning
text: Ironjaw is older than he looks. "I'm not going to be here forever. I need to know the outfit survives me."
"A council. Commanders sharing authority, checking each other's power, maintaining the mission. You would be on it."

choices.text: "I'll serve. The mission is bigger than any person."

enf_071.title: The Council
text: The Commander's Council: you, Kess, two others. Shared authority. Ironjaw advises.
First meeting. Same desk. Same map. Same coffee machine.
"First order: community clinic in Block 22 needs security. Pro bono?"
Unanimous yes. The outfit continues.

enf_072.title: Democratic Security
text: "A council is just a smaller autocracy. The outfit should be governed by the community it protects."
Ironjaw stares. "That doesn't exist."
"Neither did independent security that protects people for free. You built that. This is the next step."
Six months of transition. Messy. But the district owns its protection now.

enf_073.title: The Next Generation
text: You train the next generation. Tactical skills plus negotiation, de-escalation, ethics. A young woman named Sera stands out.
"Why do you want to do this?"
"Because someone protected me once. I want to be that person for someone else."

enf_074.title: Mentorship
text: You mentor Sera through experience. Details, council meetings, planning. Within a year, the best recruit the outfit has produced.
"She's you," Kess says.
"Better. She doesn't carry the same damage."
Grunt to Guard in eight months — fastest in the outfit's history.

choices.text: The future is in good hands.

enf_075.title: The Standard Path
text: Sera goes through the standard program. Excels. Leads her cohort through demonstrated competence. No resentment, no whispered accusations.
"The standard path built legitimacy," Kess says. "She doesn't owe her rank to you. She owns it."

enf_076.title: Ironjaw's Farewell
text: Ironjaw retires on a quiet Tuesday. No ceremony. Clears his desk. Packs a small bag.
At the door: "The outfit isn't mine anymore. It belongs to the district."
"Thank you, Captain."
The titanium smile. "Don't call me Captain. I'm retired." He walks out. The coffee machine hums.
You sit behind your desk and look at the map.

enf_077.title: Commander's Morning
text: Morning routine. The district is quieter. Not peaceful — stable. Markets without rackets. Medical facilities without fear. Children walking to school through former gang territory.
Today's priority: reports of a corporate consortium — multiple companies — planning coordinated entry. Not one corporation. Many.

enf_078.title: The Consortium
text: Six companies — water, energy, medical, manufacturing, communications, security — coordinating as a unified front. The consortium controls everything simultaneously.
Your counter: community trust, local knowledge, coordinated response. The consortium thinks it faces fragmented resistance. It faces a community that has learned to organize.

enf_079.title: Council of War
text: "Six companies. Six fronts."
"We can't fight them all," Kess says.
Mother Vasquez: "We don't fight them. We make them fight each other. Corporations are allies of convenience. Give them reason to compete, and the consortium fractures."
Not muscle. Mind.

enf_080.title: The Long Campaign
text: Months of feeding information between consortium members, amplifying competition, building community resistance. The consortium fractures. The remaining companies negotiate genuine partnerships with community oversight.
Not the defeat of corporate influence — a managed relationship. The outfit evolves from muscle to mediator.

enf_081.title: The Enforcer's Reflection
text: Block 14's roof at dusk. City lights coming on. From Grunt to Commander. From muscle to strategist to community architect.
The outfit built none of it. The outfit protected the people who built it.
Not enforcer, not guard, not commander. Protector.

enf_082.title: Full Circle
text: A new recruit at the Red Door. Same questions from the men outside. You watch from inside, remembering.
Same factory floor. Updated weapons racks — less lethal options outnumber lethal. A classroom for ethics and de-escalation.
The recruit is brought to you. Same desk, same assessment.
"Tell me," you say. "What can you do?"

enf_083.title: The Red Door Legacy
text: The recruit is promising. You assign them to Sera's squad — she's Lieutenant now. Forty-eight operators, twelve liaisons, partnerships across the district.
Tomorrow there will be threats. Tonight, the Red Door is closed and the coffee machine hums.

enf_084.title: Night Patrol
text: You still walk the district at night. Not patrol — practice. Seeing the district as residents see it.
A merchant recognizes you. "Still walking?"
"Still walking."
"Good. We see you walking, we feel safe."
The simplest version of the job: presence.

enf_085.title: Kess's Retirement
text: Kess retires. Small gathering. "I came because I needed work. I stayed because the work needed me. I leave because the work is in better hands."
"The arm — I said I lost it in a disagreement. The disagreement was with a Meridian operative dragging a twelve-year-old into a van. Best trade I ever made."
Silence. Kess nods once and walks out.

choices.text: Honor her by continuing the mission.

enf_086.title: The District at Peace
text: Relative peace. Violent crime down sixty percent. Medical access up four hundred percent. Life expectancy twenty years longer.
Something has changed. Not fixed. Better.

choices.text: Better is enough. Better is everything.

enf_087.title: The Enforcer's Code
text: Protect the vulnerable. Confront the powerful. Force as last resort. Intelligence as first resort. Stand your ground when standing matters. Trust your team. Earn your community's trust.
You post it in the training room. The recruits who internalize it become the operators who define the outfit.

enf_088.title: One More Detail
text: A routine detail — community meeting about water infrastructure. You take it yourself, to remember the work at its most basic.
Three hours. Nothing happens. The meeting improves service for two thousand residents. The protection was invisible — the highest form of the craft.

enf_089.title: The Weight of Years
text: Your body remembers every fight. You train lighter now. But the mind sharpens — strategic thinking, pattern recognition, threat assessment at their peak.
Youth brings strength. Age brings wisdom. Both serve the mission.

enf_090.title: Sera's Promotion
text: Sera promoted to Commander. Unanimous. You pin the crossed sword-and-shield to her collar.
"Any advice?"
"Trust your people. Trust the community. Trust your judgment. In that order."
"And when they conflict?"
"They will. Every day. That's the job. Not the fighting. The choosing."

enf_091.title: Elder Enforcer
text: Your role shifts to advisory. New recruits come for orientation. You tell them about Ironjaw, the Meridian betrayal, the clinic detail, the Nexus exposure.
"Protection is not a business. It's a responsibility. Forget that, and you're just muscle with a logo."

choices.text: Tell the stories. Keep the mission alive.

enf_092.title: The District Remembers
text: A mural on Block 14's wall. A figure before a Red Door, arms crossed. Not a specific person — an archetype. The Protector.
It's about the idea: protection is possible. Someone will always stand at the door.

enf_093.title: Ironjaw's Last Visit
text: Ironjaw visits. Older, less imposing. Same eyes. He walks through the expanded facility.
"It's different."
"Better?"
"Different. But the mission is intact. That's what matters."
You share terrible coffee. Neither mentions it.

enf_094.title: Passing the Torch
text: You transition from council to advisor. The same shift Ironjaw made. On your last day, you clear the desk.
The desk is just a desk. What it held is in good hands.

enf_095.title: The Evening Walk
text: Children play in corridors that used to be combat zones. A young operator spots you. "Evening walk, Commander?"
"Just a walk. Not Commander anymore."
"You'll always be Commander, sir."

enf_096.title: The Red Door at Night
text: The Red Door. Repainted — still red, cleaner shade. Behind it, a new class training. Same sounds you heard on your first day.
The Red Door is a promise. Someone will always answer.
You touch the door. Cold metal. Behind it, the future trains.

choices.text: Leave the door to the next generation.

enf_097.title: The Protector's Truth
text: Protection is not about strength. It's about presence. Showing up, consistently, where vulnerability exists.
The district doesn't need heroes. It needs people who show up.
Protection is a practice, not a profession. You do it every day, or you don't do it at all.

enf_098.title: Home
text: Home: small apartment in Block 14. On the desk: the ceramic shield pin and the crossed sword-and-shield. Two objects representing a career, a mission, a life.
You watch the lights. Dinner sounds. Conversation. The hum of maintained infrastructure. The sounds of a protected community.

enf_099.title: The Enforcer's Watch
text: The watch continues. Not your watch — the watch. Operators on patrol. Guards on detail. Commanders on strategy.
Sera sits behind the desk. The weight passes. The mission continues.
Somewhere, a young person reads: PROTECTION NEEDED. GOOD PEOPLE. ASK INSIDE.
The wording has changed. The door hasn't.

enf_100.title: The Red Door Endures
text: In the Lower District, they tell the story of the Red Door. Not as legend — as fact. A door painted red, behind which people trained to protect. Not for profit. Not for power. Because protection is what communities need.
The Enforcers built something that outlasted them. An organization that evolved from muscle to mission, from protection racket to community institution.
The Red Door is still there. Still red. Still open. New voices learn old lessons: protect the vulnerable, confront the powerful, stand your ground, trust your people.
The district is not safe. But it is protected — imperfectly, persistently.
The Red Door endures. The watch continues. The district breathes.
